title: Project Kennel Trust Manifest
description: Cryptographic boundaries and execution triggers for host-side tooling. A confined Project Kennel workload cannot see or rewrite this file (it is masked inside the kennel, along with the .trust-manifest.d blob store beside it), so a host IDE can trust its pins to detect workspace-trigger tampering (T2.8). v2 carries a per-trigger record so a review can show a diff against the pinned blob and revert can restore a tampered trigger.

generator.description: The tool that generated or last updated this manifest.

execution.description: Defines what host tooling is allowed to execute.

triggers.description: Maps each relative trigger path to its pinned record. Host tools must refuse to execute a listed file whose on-disk SHA-256 differs from its record's sha256.

kind.description: A regular file whose bytes are pinned (content), or a symlink whose target is pinned (symlink).

sha256.description: For a content trigger, the pinned content hash 'sha256:<64-hex>' (the blob's address in .trust-manifest.d). Empty for a symlink.

target.description: For a symlink trigger, the pinned link target. Absent for a content trigger.

mode.description: The file mode as four octal digits (permission plus setuid/setgid/sticky), preserved across a revert.

pattern.description: The catalogue entry that matched this path (provenance); 'builtin' for the compiled default set.

pinned.description: How the pin was established: 'compile' or 'review'.

boundaries.description: Negative trust spaces. Host tools must treat these paths as no-exec.

untrusted_paths.description: List of relative paths or globs that are strictly untrusted.
